title: Undo{Follow} wipes all followers by actor URI, no signature required
description:
server.rs:677-703: The Undo handler deletes from the followers table keyed on `actor_uri` taken directly from the request body. No signature is required (see broadside-0e74.1).

Attack: POST to /inbox with no Signature header:
```json
{"type":"Undo","actor":"https://victim.example/users/alice","object":{"type":"Follow","actor":"https://victim.example/users/alice"}}
```

This executes: `DELETE FROM followers WHERE actor_uri = 'https://victim.example/users/alice'`

An attacker can enumerate or guess follower URIs (the outbox leaks follower info indirectly; follower list is publicly scrapable from the fediverse) and surgically remove specific followers or, if they can guess or enumerate URIs, run a loop to wipe the entire followers table.

Even with signature verification enforced, this logic has a secondary flaw: it uses `activity["object"]["actor"]` rather than `activity["actor"]`, meaning a signed actor A could send Undo{Follow} claiming object.actor is B, and delete B's follower entry.

The comment at line 678 flags this but the code is still live and unconditionally deletes.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
